steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The structure to the left is an independent clause: “Philosopher Peter Kivy was a leading figure in musical [word].” To the right, after “itself,” is another independent clause: “Kivy's approach to the study of music was decidedly formalist.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for sentence boundaries
steps.content:
A period properly separates two independent clauses into distinct sentences. When placed after “aesthetics,” the second sentence correctly begins with a participial phrase (“As evidenced by...”), followed by a comma and the main independent clause.

Options A, B, and C fail to properly separate the two independent clauses—either omitting necessary punctuation or creating ungrammatical formations.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
